articles.title: Oman announces temporary maritime corridor to bypass Hormuz blockade
articles.summary: Oman announces temporary maritime corridor to bypass Hormuz blockade Oman said on Tuesday that it had coordinated with the International Maritime Organisation (IMO) to provide a temporary maritime corridor for vessels seeking to transit through the Strait of Hormuz, Oman's state news agency reported. It added that vessels wishing to pass through would need to coordinate with the IMO and Omani authorities.

articles.title: UN report: 80 percent of Gaza living in dire conditions
articles.summary: UN report: 80 percent of Gaza living in dire conditions Nearly 1.7 million displaced Palestinians - about 80 percent of Gaza’s population - are living with acute shortages of water, shelter and basic services, according to an assessment of displacement sites from the UN’s Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs. A spokesperson for the UN chief added that at least 59,000 shelters house more than eight people each, and around 38,500 people are sleeping in the open, while an estimated 600,000 people lack sufficient access to drinking water. Local sources said that Israeli soldiers, accompanied by heavy machinery, knocked down a two-story building which was home to two families, including children and elderly residents, who have been left homeless following the demolition. articles.title: US Senate votes to halt Iran war
articles.summary: US Senate votes to halt Iran war The Republican-majority US Senate backed legislation on Tuesday to stop military action against Iran, reflecting the growing unpopularity of the war as peace negotiations continue. The Senate voted 50-48 in favour of the resolution, which passed the House of Representatives in early June.  Polling suggests that only one in four Americans believe that the Iran war was worth the cost to the US, affecting Trump’s popularity, which has fallen to record low levels to match the worst recorded in his second term.

articles.title: SpaceX Set to Raise $25 Billion of Bonds in Debut Offer
articles.summary: Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and AI conglomerate is set to raise $25 billion of bonds in five parts on Tuesday, in its debut bond offer, and is paying a relatively wide premium over Treasuries to get the deal done, people familiar with the matter said. articles.title: US temporarily eases restrictions on Iran’s World Cup team
articles.summary: US temporarily eases restrictions on Iran’s World Cup team The United States has eased restrictions on Iran’s football team for the World Cup, allowing them to travel into the US two days before their next match rather than one, according to the US Department of Homeland Security (DHS). The restrictions placed on the players had led the Iranian coach to say that his country’s was “the most oppressed team in the whole World Cup”. articles.title: Without missiles Iran would have ended up ‘like Gaza’, Iranian president says
articles.summary: Without missiles Iran would have ended up ‘like Gaza’, Iranian president says Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said on Tuesday that if Iran did not have its missile defence programme, it would have ended up “razed like Gaza” in the US-Israeli war. “If the missiles we have for our defence did not exist, Israel and the United States would have razed Iran like Gaza,” Pezeshkian said, reiterating that Iran’s defensive capabilities would not be negotiated “under any circumstances”.

articles.title: Most Americans say Iran war not worth costs, Trump approval falls to match lowest in second term
articles.summary: Most Americans say Iran war not worth costs, Trump approval falls to match lowest in second term A majority of Americans (52 percent) now say that the US war on Iran was not worth its costs, while only 24 percent agree that it was, according to a Reuters/IPSOS poll on Tuesday. The poll also showed that only 23 percent of Americans say that the US is in a stronger position with Iran after the war, whereas 35 percent consider the US position to be weaker. articles.summary: Trump: Iran is wrong about no plans for IAEA to inspect nuclear sites US President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that Iran was wrong about there not being any plans for International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) officials arriving to inspect Iran’s damaged nuclear sites.  Trump added that the UN nuclear watchdog inspectors would be on the ground in Iran to carry out this mission at the “appropriate time”. Since 2 March, seven blue helmets with the UN peacekeeping mission have been killed by Israel in Lebanon. articles.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire talks a ‘train wreck’, Israeli ambassador to US says
articles.summary: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire talks a ‘train wreck’, Israeli ambassador to US says Israel's ambassador to the US, Yechiel Leiter, described the fifth round of US-mediated ceasefire negotiations with Lebanon as a “train wreck” in a statement on Tuesday. “I fear that the concept of ‘deconfliction’ is misplaced. The only issue is Hezbollah. Hezbollah must be defeated and removed from the equation,” Leiter stated, adding that, instead, the Lebanese armed group “feels stronger and bolder”. articles.title: Sharif warns of ‘spoilers’ attempting to sabotage US-Iran deal
articles.summary: Sharif warns of ‘spoilers’ attempting to sabotage US-Iran deal Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif warned that some actors are trying to derail the process towards achieving a ceasefire deal between the US and Iran. “There are spoilers all over the world who want to scuttle this peace deal,” Sharif said, without mentioning any specific countries or parties involved. “They don’t want the Iranian nation, a great nation, to come out of the ashes of war and touch the zenith of glory,” he added.

articles.title: Iran will never negotiate defensive ability, president says
articles.summary: Iran will never negotiate defensive ability, president says Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said that Iran will never negotiate their defensive ability with any country. Iran’s ballistic missiles programme was not in the Memorandum of Understanding signed with the US and “will never be”, Pezeshkian told reporters in a press conference in Islamabad, after meeting with Pakistan’s prime minister on Tuesday. He added that he believes regional peace and stability will only be achieved through honest discussions and intraregional cooperation.
